tagline: A fixed-rank hunter economy is broken by one player running an infinite growth protocol.
visualizationReason: Solo Leveling is structurally a control network: gates route crises, institutions allocate scarce S-rank capacity, and Jinwoo's shadow authority rewires dependency edges faster than legacy power blocs can adapt.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: If the world is a dungeon, I'll clear every floor.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_WARN]: STATIC-RANK UNIVERSE BREACHED — SINGLE NODE SCALING WITHOUT CAP

introductionSummary: Solo Leveling begins in a world where hunter rank is mostly permanent and failure inside gates spills catastrophe into civilian space. Sung Jinwoo's System breaks that fixed-cap law by introducing continuous stat growth, forced quests, and post-battle unit conversion. As Jinwoo transitions from fragile raider to sovereign commander, the core question shifts from personal survival to total network control over planetary threat flow.

aiInsights.casual: Most hunters in Solo Leveling are stuck with the power they awaken with, but Jinwoo gets a game-like System that lets him keep leveling forever. That one exception lets him leap past normal S-rank limits and eventually command an army built from defeated enemies.
aiInsights.deep: Solo Leveling runs a static-cap labor market until the System introduces a single adaptive node with compounding throughput. Jinwoo's loop combines XP scaling, deterministic penalty enforcement, and recursive shadow conversion, producing a positive feedback architecture that outcompetes guild, state, and monarch pressure. Once authority migrates from interface-mediated 'player' state to native Shadow Monarch permissions, the prior equilibrium becomes mathematically unstable.

powerSystem.name: Awakening Rank Lock
powerSystem.subtitle: Static Mana Ceiling
powerSystem.loreDesc: Hunters typically awaken once and remain trapped near that rank for life, making power distribution brutally unequal.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A near-immutable class-assignment model with minimal vertical progression after initialization.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Early gate teams treat E-rank Jinwoo as expendable because rank ceilings are assumed permanent.

powerSystem.name: The System
powerSystem.subtitle: Adaptive Growth Interface
powerSystem.loreDesc: A hidden quest UI forces Jinwoo through lethal milestones, grants rewards, and rewrites his limits as if reality were an RPG.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A supervised progression kernel that enforces tasks, grants XP, and performs controlled host scaling under strict failure penalties.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: The Double Dungeon contract initializes Jinwoo as the only active player in a non-leveling universe.

powerSystem.name: Shadow Extraction
powerSystem.subtitle: Post-Combat Unit Conversion
powerSystem.loreDesc: Defeated enemies can be raised as loyal shadows, turning each victory into permanent force growth.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A recursive conversion pipeline that transforms hostile losses into retained deployable assets.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Elite enemies like Igris and Beru become command units instead of one-time raid clears.

powerSystem.name: Monarch/Ruler Sovereignty
powerSystem.subtitle: Cross-Dimensional Authority
powerSystem.loreDesc: Higher beings wage war through vessels, gates, and planetary-scale interventions that dwarf ordinary hunter politics.
powerSystem.systemDesc: Top-layer permission architecture where sovereignty transfer and vessel compatibility dictate strategic dominance.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Jinwoo's inheritance of Shadow Monarch authority replaces temporary interface scaffolding with native control.

rankings.topTierLore: Cosmic authorities whose conflict determines civilization-scale survival outcomes.
rankings.topTierSystem: Root-level actors with cross-dimensional privileges and strategic override capability.

tiers.name: National Level / Vessel-Class Hunter
tiers.loreDesc: Rare hunters or vessels who can swing international war scenarios.
tiers.systemDesc: Highest human-adjacent throughput nodes with exceptional deterrence value.

tiers.name: S-Rank Hunter
tiers.loreDesc: Top conventional combat specialists and guild anchors.
tiers.systemDesc: Upper-bound standard class before sovereign layers activate.

tiers.name: A-E Rank Hunters
tiers.loreDesc: Main hunter labor pool with steep survivability gaps by rank.
tiers.systemDesc: Fixed-cap operational workforce with limited progression elasticity.

characters.name: Sung Jin-Woo
characters.title: The Player / Shadow Monarch
characters.loreBio: Formerly the weakest E-rank, Jinwoo survives the Double Dungeon and becomes the sole hunter with limitless growth before inheriting monarch authority.
characters.systemBio: Primary mutable node in a static-cap ecosystem, compounding stats, skills, and converted units until systemic dominance.
characters.signatureMoment: Transitioning from System player to sovereign commander and deploying shadow legions at scale.
characters.description: Sung Jin-Woo is a  from Solo Leveling.

characters.title: The S-Rank Blade
characters.loreBio: An elite swordswoman whose acute mana sensitivity makes Jinwoo's abnormal profile impossible to ignore.
characters.systemBio: High-precision strike node and anomaly detector validating Jinwoo's deviation from legacy hunter baselines.
characters.signatureMoment: Recognizing Jinwoo as a category-breaker while top guilds still model him as a conventional outlier.

characters.title: The Logistics Anchor

characters.systemBio: Support node converting capital and coordination into sustained uptime for Jinwoo's progression loop.
characters.signatureMoment: Backing Jinwoo's independent path when established guild systems hesitate.

characters.title: The Institutional Gatekeeper

characters.systemBio: Policy-layer stabilizer managing classification, national risk, and escalation around a rapidly mutating combat node.
characters.signatureMoment: Choosing cooperation over containment as Jinwoo's capabilities exceed institutional playbooks.

characters.title: The Beast Guild Commander
characters.loreBio: A veteran S-rank guild master who quickly realizes Jinwoo is destabilizing accepted power assumptions.
characters.systemBio: Experienced benchmark node for pre-System S-rank ceilings and coalition response behavior.
characters.signatureMoment: Recalibrating guild-level risk models after Jinwoo's solo performance data.

characters.title: The Ultimate Soldier
characters.loreBio: Hunters Guild's top fire mage and one of Korea's major raid strategists.
characters.systemBio: High-output ranged damage node representing conventional top-tier raid doctrine.
characters.signatureMoment: Serving as a baseline for how far standard S-rank specialization can go without System-style mutation.

characters.title: The Operations Enforcer
characters.loreBio: A disciplined association officer who executes crisis response and verification tasks across Korea's gate emergencies.
characters.systemBio: Execution-layer command node translating policy into rapid field deployment under uncertainty.
characters.signatureMoment: Coordinating emergency response when gate incidents outrun normal staffing assumptions.

characters.name: Lee Joo-Hee
characters.title: The Trauma-Bound Healer
characters.loreBio: A healer marked by repeated raid trauma whose early partnership with Jinwoo frames the human cost of the gate economy.
characters.systemBio: Support-recovery node illustrating attrition pressure and psychological failure risk in frontline operations.
characters.signatureMoment: Withdrawing from frontline operations after cumulative shock, highlighting non-power bottlenecks in hunter sustainability.

characters.name: Thomas Andre
characters.title: National-Level Guildmaster
characters.loreBio: Leader of the Scavenger Guild whose overwhelming force represents the pre-monarch ceiling of human deterrence.
characters.systemBio: External benchmark node for global S-rank governance; his presence tests whether Jinwoo is merely exceptional or structurally game-breaking.
characters.signatureMoment: After Jeju and later crises, his acknowledgement of Jinwoo signals a global reindexing of hunter authority.
characters.description: Thomas Andre is a  from Solo Leveling.

characters.name: Ashborn
characters.title: Original Shadow Monarch
characters.loreBio: The primordial Shadow Monarch whose legacy defines sovereign succession and the architecture Jinwoo ultimately inherits.
characters.systemBio: Root-authority predecessor node that explains how monarch permissions can transfer without collapsing command continuity.
characters.signatureMoment: His inheritance chain reframes Jinwoo's rise as succession into an existing sovereign stack, not a random miracle.
characters.description: Ashborn is a  from Solo Leveling.

relationships.loreDesc: Their alliance strengthens as Jinwoo moves from unknown hunter to critical defender.
relationships.systemDesc: High-trust combat edge linking sovereign-scale growth with elite strike precision.

relationships.loreDesc: Jin-Ho supports Jinwoo before the world understands his trajectory.
relationships.systemDesc: Logistics dependency edge that sustains early growth throughput.

relationships.loreDesc: Go Gun-Hee offers institutional guidance as Jinwoo enters national-level conflict zones.
relationships.systemDesc: Governance-to-asset advisory edge reducing policy friction around an unstable high-value node.

relationships.loreDesc: Baek initially measures himself against Jinwoo before shifting toward strategic respect.
relationships.systemDesc: Comparative performance edge between established S-rank ceilings and accelerated scaling.

relationships.loreDesc: Choi's elite firepower highlights what conventional raid doctrine can and cannot solve.
relationships.systemDesc: Doctrine edge contrasting static specialization against adaptive multiphase growth.

relationships.loreDesc: Woo executes association strategy under Gun-Hee's leadership.
relationships.systemDesc: Command edge linking policy authority to tactical implementation.

relationships.loreDesc: Their early partnership shows Jinwoo's pre-sovereign vulnerability and human stakes.
relationships.systemDesc: Legacy support edge from low-tier survival phase before scaling breakaway.

relationships.loreDesc: Cha increasingly aligns with Jinwoo's methods as older systems prove insufficient.
relationships.systemDesc: Adoption edge where elite institutional talent migrates toward the emergent dominant node.

relationships.loreDesc: A top global hunter initially sizes Jinwoo up as a contender before recognizing a new tier entirely.
relationships.systemDesc: Comparator edge between legacy national-level throughput and system-accelerated sovereign growth.

relationships.loreDesc: Ashborn's legacy positions Jinwoo as the active inheritor of shadow sovereignty against rival monarch pressure.
relationships.systemDesc: Terminal conflict edge where monarch-layer command systems directly collide.

relationships.loreDesc: Both veterans prioritize coordinated defense over guild prestige during global escalations.
relationships.systemDesc: Institutional cooperation link that reduces response fragmentation across national command structures.

factions.name: Korean Hunters Association
factions.loreDesc: The regulatory body that certifies hunters, allocates gate responses, and triages national risk.
factions.systemDesc: Governance layer coordinating classification, deployment, and incident escalation.

factions.name: Major Korean Guilds
factions.loreDesc: Top guild organizations that carry most frontline raid burden.
factions.systemDesc: Distributed combat cluster with strong elites but limited adaptation speed.

factions.name: Dungeon Monster Forces
factions.loreDesc: Gate-spawned enemies that threaten civilian collapse when containment fails.
factions.systemDesc: Continuous hostile event stream generated by unresolved dimensional instances.

factions.name: Rulers
factions.loreDesc: Higher-order defenders intervening indirectly through vessels and strategic timing.
factions.systemDesc: External supervisory layer using selective empowerment rather than constant direct control.

factions.name: Shadow Army
factions.loreDesc: Jinwoo's expanding force of converted shadows that changes battle math every time it deploys.
factions.systemDesc: Recursive force-multiplication network with centralized command authority.

factions.name: International Hunter Authorities
factions.loreDesc: National-level associations and governments coordinating treaty-scale responses to gates and sovereign incursions.
factions.systemDesc: Macro-governance layer that allocates elite labor, negotiates cross-border raid authority, and updates deterrence doctrine.

rules.name: Awakening Sets Baseline Capacity
rules.subtitle: Most hunters cannot truly level
rules.loreConsequence: A weak awakening can become a lifelong death sentence in high-rank gates.
rules.systemEquivalent: Near-immutable class initialization creates rigid power stratification.

rules.name: Gate Timeout Equals Civilian Disaster
rules.subtitle: Uncleared dungeons break containment
rules.loreConsequence: Failed raids let monsters spill into cities, causing mass casualties.
rules.systemEquivalent: Instance timeout triggers uncontrolled external spawn events.

rules.name: System Tasks Are Non-Optional
rules.subtitle: Failure invokes punishment
rules.loreConsequence: Jinwoo cannot safely ignore quests without risking immediate lethal penalties.
rules.systemEquivalent: Mandatory scheduler with hard fail-state enforcement to maintain adaptation velocity.

rules.name: Shadow Recruitment Requires Defeat
rules.subtitle: Command is earned in combat
rules.loreConsequence: Jinwoo only expands his army by surviving and winning dangerous fights.
rules.systemEquivalent: Unit conversion locked behind successful combat resolution and authority checks.

rules.name: Rollback Resources Are Finite
rules.subtitle: Time reset is not infinite
rules.loreConsequence: Using cosmic reset tools can save the world once more, but each use permanently drains the option.
rules.systemEquivalent: Global rollback utility with limited charges and irreversible depletion.

rules.name: Sovereign Conflict Overrides National Borders
rules.loreSubtitle: No nation can solo a monarch war
rules.loreConsequence: Once monarchs fully mobilize, even top guild states cannot contain damage within domestic lines.
rules.systemEquivalent: Endgame threats invalidate local optimization and force transnational command pooling.
rules.subtitle: Structural Escalation Constraint

counterplay.mechanic: Guilds and association teams must clear instances before timeout thresholds.
counterplay.loreDesc: Every operation is a race against catastrophe.
counterplay.systemDesc: Response orchestration interrupts hostile spillover pipeline.

counterplay.mechanic: Jinwoo accumulates repeatable growth that bypasses ordinary awakening limits.
counterplay.loreDesc: The old rule that power cannot grow is forcibly invalidated.
counterplay.systemDesc: Adaptive progression subroutine defeats hard-cap class model.

counterplay.mechanic: Defeated enemies become Jinwoo's reusable units.
counterplay.loreDesc: Losses stop being losses when the fallen can be re-commanded.
counterplay.systemDesc: Combat outputs recursively expand force inventory.

counterplay.mechanic: Jinwoo coordinates multi-unit shadow tactics beyond normal hunter specialization.
counterplay.loreDesc: Standard elite hunters cannot mirror monarch-level command bandwidth.
counterplay.systemDesc: Authority-layer upgrade outperforms static specialist templates.

counterplay.mechanic: A final timeline reset creates a cleaner branch for decisive threat neutralization.
counterplay.loreDesc: When direct victory fails, time itself is used as emergency counterplay.
counterplay.systemDesc: Checkpoint restore reopens execution path under better strategic state.

anomalies.name: Second Awakening Mutation
anomalies.loreDesc: Jinwoo's growth directly contradicts the foundational assumption of hunter society.
anomalies.systemDesc: Exclusive mutable-host protocol breaches static-cap universe constraints.

anomalies.name: Persistent Authority After UI Dissolution
anomalies.ruleViolated: System interface should be required for command access
anomalies.loreDesc: Jinwoo retains functional command even after the visible System layer fades.
anomalies.systemDesc: Core authority kernel persists after presentation layer decommission.

anomalies.name: Selective Memory Across Rollback
anomalies.ruleViolated: Global reset should erase prior-state awareness
anomalies.loreDesc: High-tier actors can retain traces that ordinary timeline participants lose.
anomalies.systemDesc: Rollback process exhibits partial immunity at elevated ontological layers.

causalEvents.name: Double Dungeon Contract
causalEvents.trigger: Jinwoo survives a hidden sacrificial trial
causalEvents.consequence: The System initializes and designates him as the sole leveling player.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The weakest hunter is forcibly converted into the world's lone growth exception.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Bootstrap event creating mutable-node insertion in static-cap architecture.

causalEvents.name: High-Risk Gate Escalation
causalEvents.trigger: System quests drive Jinwoo into successively harder dungeon environments
causalEvents.consequence: He rapidly surpasses ordinary S-rank output and compounds shadow inventory.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Repeated lethal clears transform Jinwoo from anomaly to apex field actor.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Accelerated XP + conversion loop yields nonlinear throughput growth.

causalEvents.name: Monarch Authority Inheritance
causalEvents.consequence: Temporary System scaffolding gives way to native sovereign command privileges.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The player phase ends and sovereign phase begins.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Control transfer from mediated interface to root authority runtime.

causalEvents.name: Final Rollback Branch
causalEvents.trigger: Global losses approach collapse in late war state
causalEvents.consequence: A finite reset produces a final route for decisive threat neutralization.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Humanity receives one last chance to avoid terminal defeat.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Limited checkpoint restore generates improved endgame execution branch.

causalEvents.name: Jeju Raid Reorders Global Hunter Hierarchy
causalEvents.trigger: Ant-level catastrophe forces multinational intervention under severe casualty pressure.
causalEvents.consequence: Jinwoo's intervention shifts trust, deterrence planning, and recruitment assumptions worldwide.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The Jeju operation exposes how brittle legacy S-rank planning is until Jinwoo's shadow force flips the board.
causalEvents.systemDesc: A regional raid becomes a global reindex event: authority migrates toward whoever can deliver repeatable high-end clearance.

systemQuestions.question: Why is Jinwoo's growth a systemic anomaly instead of just being talented?
systemQuestions.answer: Because ordinary awakenings are fixed-cap while Jinwoo alone runs a repeatable stat-and-skill progression loop.

systemQuestions.question: What is the practical function of the System?
systemQuestions.answer: It is a coercive adaptation framework that conditions Jinwoo for sovereign authority while preventing early self-destruction.

systemQuestions.question: Why do gates create constant strategic instability?
systemQuestions.answer: Any uncleared gate can breach into civilian space, so institutions must continuously triage finite elite response capacity.

systemQuestions.question: Why is node-graph the best renderer for Solo Leveling?
systemQuestions.answer: The key dynamics are dependency and control edges between hunters, institutions, and emergent sovereign authority, not a single duel ladder.

systemQuestions.question: What makes shadow extraction strategically overpowered?
systemQuestions.answer: It converts defeated enemies into permanent assets, so each win grows future force capacity instead of only preserving current state.

systemQuestions.question: Why does finite rollback matter in the endgame?
systemQuestions.answer: It is a limited-use recovery mechanism that can reopen a lost war state once, but cannot be relied on indefinitely.

systemQuestions.question: Why do national-level hunters still matter after Jinwoo breaks scaling limits?
systemQuestions.answer: They remain institutional stabilizers and deterrence anchors while the world transitions from state-era defense to sovereign-era conflict.

hero.microHook: Track control edges before reading motives.
hero.thesis: Solo Leveling is structurally a control network: gates route crises, institutions allocate scarce S-rank capacity, and Jinwoo's shadow auth…
